title: Doctor Izzy's Busy Hospital Day
summary: Doctor Izzy manages a hectic day at her hospital, treating a diverse array of patients with everything from long toenails to sprained wings. With a steady hand and a collection of specialized tools, she performs rapid tonsillectomies, interprets X-rays, and administers vitamins to those in need. After a long day of documenting infections and performing miraculous recoveries, she successfully discharges every patient, ensuring everyone returns home healthy and healed.

highlightQuote.text: Puke. So that one puked. And tonsils out. Tail infection. Sprained ankle. Skateboard ouchie. Sprained ankle. Tonsils out. Puke. Poop. Ate a not crunchy nut. Ate a rock. That's my message.

transcript.text: Oh, you tripped on your foot. I kept tripping on my own knee on the way to the hospital. You did? Okay. Well, lay down and Doctor Izzy will examine you. Okay, what tools do you need for the exam, Doctor Izzy?
transcript.speaker: Dad
Izzy
transcript.text: Need to look at her ear, the stethoscope, medicine.
Okay, patient is ready. Oh, what do you see there?
transcript.speaker: Dad
Izzy
transcript.text: Definitely... definitely kept tripping on the knee.
Kept tripping on the knee, yeah. Well, what do you see is going on with that knee there?
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: I don't know. Trying to get his toenails clipped.
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: Oh, that's an important thing to do.
I can see why you were tripping on your own knee because that was because your toenail was too long.
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: Oh my goodness, I thought it was because of my knee. Hey, I think you solved my problem. Thank you. Thank you, Doctor Izzy. Okay, who's next in our triage?
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: Mine... um, my fur, my fur. My fur is too long.
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: Your fur is too long. Oh dear. And what problems does that cause you to have? Does it go in front of your eyes so you can't see? And is it kind of itchy? Oh, well, I think we can help you with that. Doctor Izzy, what do you recommend?
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: Ear-scope.
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: Ah, okay. You want me to use it? All right, I'll have a look in your ear. Oh, your ear looks okay. It's just covered with hair. What's next?
Cutting the fur. There, your fur was too long. I snipped it for you. It isn't over your eyes.
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: Thank you! Wow, I can see everything.
Tonsils out.
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: Oh, this is a major operation, Doctor Izzy. Now first we should tell the patient what's going to happen because she looks a little nervous.
First there's going to be some music to put you to sleep the whole time and then we're going to have to look at your tonsils. So say ah.
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: Ah. Okay, let's get the music going.
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: All right, that's enough music. Now how do we prepare the patient for the operation?
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: She's already prepared. Now we should just... they're out! They're out!
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: Oh, the tonsils are out! That was a fast surgery, Doctor Izzy. Oh, and then we move the patient to recovery. How's she doing?
Good. I sprained my wings.
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: Oh, you sprained your wings. Ah, I'm sorry to hear that. It must be very painful. Now please go into the exam room and Doctor Izzy will be in shortly to examine you. Oh, come on in, Doctor Izzy.
transcript.speaker: Dad
Izzy
transcript.text: Thank you, nurse. Oh, forgot to bring the speaker. Oh, no cats in the hospital. Okay.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And the patient says, um, excuse me, I'm waiting for my sprained wing to be treated by a doctor. Are you available?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yeah, just this song to make you fall asleep.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, I see. I don't think that's going to make me fall asleep because... zzz... oh, Doctor Izzy, I think the patient is asleep. Well, do we need to X-ray that wing or just examine it?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Took one X-ray.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Okay. Here's the X-ray, Doctor Izzy. What do you see? How's it look?
It looks like a sprained wing to me.
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: A sprained wing, I see. And what do you recommend for a treatment for that sprained wing? Just some pain medicine or maybe a bandage or a sling?
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: Just the medicine. It was just a few more X-rays. I sprained my tail. I sprained my tail right there.
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: Now I notice you've got a big flower bow on your tail.
No, no, no, that's my tail warmer.
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: Oh, your tail warmer. Do you think that might have caused your sprain?
transcript.speaker: Dad
Izzy
transcript.text: No. I slipped on the ice when I was ice skating a few months ago.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, you slipped on the ice when you were ice skating. I see. Well, come on in the exam room and Doctor Izzy will examine you.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I'll have to sleep like that, on my side. Doesn't look good to me. I'll get some... here's a screw, we need some vitamins. Here's your vitamin.
Okay, thank you, but my tail still hurts a lot. Do you think that tail might need a cast or a bandage or maybe even a surgery?
transcript.speaker: Dad
Izzy
transcript.text: I think I'll get a bandage from somewhere. I wrote down a few surgeries, now I'm going to write down the next surgeries. I've written down all the infections that we had.
Oh, could you read your medical note to me, please, Doctor Izzy?
transcript.speaker: Dad
Izzy
transcript.text: Puke. So that one puked. And tonsils out. Tail infection. Sprained ankle. Skateboard ouchie. Sprained ankle. Tonsils out. Puke. Poop. Ate a not crunchy nut. Ate a rock. That's my message.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Wow, you had a busy day seeing patients, Doctor Izzy, but I think you healed everyone.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I didn't heal everybody. Only one patient's left. Zoe Rodda. Oh, I sprained my tail.
Zoe Rodda, okay. Well, come on into the exam room. You sprained your tail too? Okay, well, lie down on your side so that Doctor Izzy can examine your tail.
transcript.speaker: Dad
Izzy
transcript.text: Fixed already! I just used my doctor tools.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, thanks! What was wrong with it? How did you fix it so fast? Wow, Doctor Izzy, you're a very skillful healer.
Now they're all discharged. Dad, can I bring these ones home and you can bring the rest?
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: Yep, we can discharge everyone because they're all better. Okay, we'll bring everybody home from the hospital.
transcript.speaker: Dad

characters.name: Dr. Izzy
characters.description: The dedicated physician at the animal hospital who treats Buddy, wielding a specialized blue earoscope with practiced precision to diagnose her patients.

characters.name: Dad
characters.description: Kitty’s father remains a pillar of steady eccentricity, an unflappable co-pilot and accommodating listener who balances technical logistics with a patient support for his daughter’s restless need for activity. He manages mission equipment with the same meticulous intensity he once brought to scanning the midnight undergrowth for missing cats, his stoicism famously underscored by the irony of a nagging knee complaint—diagnosed as overgrown toenails—that never wavers his resolve.

characters.name: Furry Patient
characters.description: An animal with excessively long fur that obstructed its vision and caused itchiness.

characters.name: Tonsil Patient
characters.description: A nervous patient who underwent a major tonsillectomy while listening to soothing music.

characters.name: Bird
characters.description: High within the foliage, the creature’s rhythmic chirping signals a steady recovery from a sprained wing that once required clinical X-rays and a precise regimen of specialized pain medicine.

characters.name: Mouse
characters.description: An ice-skating enthusiast who sprained its tail and wears a tail warmer.

characters.name: Zoe Rodda
characters.description: The final patient of the day who received treatment for a sprained tail.

places.name: The Hospital
places.description: The clinical hub for surgeries and examinations, now transformed into a hushed, antiseptic sanctuary where Mama and her newborn recover amidst the sterile hum of life-sustaining machinery.

places.name: Exam Room
places.description: A specialized room within the hospital used for diagnostics and consultations.

places.name: Recovery Room
places.description: The area where patients rest and recover following their surgeries.

places.name: Ice Skating Rink
places.description: The location where the mouse suffered a tail injury during a slip.
